channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Cariboo Signals - Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [XDA Developers] Please stop relying on the Internet for your smart home devices and [XDA Developers] I blocked all cloud access from my smart home for a week to see what still works, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, fiber, telemedicine, solar, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Please stop relying on the Internet for you...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I blocked all cloud access from my smart ho...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Feds commit $70.4M for worker traini...
  • U.S. News & World Report: [U.S. News & World Report] Telemedicine Not Closing the Ment...

Additional sources provided:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I self-hosted everything for a year, and th...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] EDITOR: Alternative available for Yell...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] What Can You Do with Used Plastic and 3D Printers...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] TerraPower Nuclear Reactor in Wyoming Gets...
  • The Globe and Mail: [The Globe and Mail] Tumbler Ridge shooting highlights rural...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] 6 things I've learned after a decade of using ...

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [XDA Developers] KDE Plasma 6.7.0 will make managing your printers a lot easier and [The Economic Times] Odisha mobilises over 5,000 MSMEs in clean energy push at Global Cleantech Expo, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: satellite, telemedicine, AI, infrastructure, maintenance.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] KDE Plasma 6.7.0 will make managing your pr...
  • The Economic Times: [The Economic Times] Odisha mobilises over 5,000 MSMEs in cl...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] 7 ways AI can make remote work more productive ...

  • Neowin: [Neowin] Microsoft announces new ESU programs for more versi...

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Electric Jeep With Modified Prius Hardware
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Forget solid-state batteries – researchers have ...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] B.C. paramedics union reaches agreement wit...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I tested the 'Death Star of speakers' for a wee...
  • TipRanks: [TipRanks] Nscale Targets Telecom Opportunities in Sovereign...

item.title: Cariboo Signals - Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Boing Boing] Amazing camera trap footage represents hope for endangered tigers and [Android Authority] Amazfit just launched its biggest watch yet, and it’s packed for navigating, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Is advancing technology genuinely improving backcountry safety accessibility for rural communities, or is it creating new barriers and false confidence? Topics include: Technology equity and affordability barriers, Rural connectivity and infrastructure challenges, Training program sustainability and access, Technology literacy versus traditional backcountry skills, Community-based implementation strategies.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Forget the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 — the Am...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] What You Need to Know About Avalanche Safe...

  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Amazfit just launched its biggest watch ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Another Storm Complicates Efforts to Recov...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. skiers cautioned after split...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] After Avalanche Warnings, a Sierra Nevada ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] In Lake Tahoe Avalanche Recovery, the Safe...
  • Lifehacker: [Lifehacker] This iPhone Feature Can Save Your Life in an Em...

item.title: Cariboo Signals - Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Nautilus] Space Age Technology Reveals Secrets of Bronze Age Sword and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. quietly suspends major affordable housing fund, leaving proponents holding the bag, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can Indigenous communities use technology to shift power relationships and control innovation processes, or are they being forced to innovate around systemic problems while facing extractive timelines? Topics include: Digital preservation and cultural knowledge documentation, Critical minerals extraction vs Indigenous consent processes, Indigenous-controlled technology infrastructure requirements, Scale mismatches between community governance and global commodity timelines, Technology as a tool for shifting power relationships rather than just responding to them. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] As B.C. stokes its economic engine, Eby says r... Additional sources provided: 6. Techdirt: [Techdirt] Preserving The Web Is Not The Problem. Losing It ... 7. item.title: Cariboo Signals - Community Tech And Governance
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] Independent Investigations Office determining if mandate met for Tumbler Ridge investigation and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Former B.C. Conservative leader will not take another leadership run, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can AI-driven infrastructure management systems enhance rural governance while maintaining democratic accountability and community-centered decision-making? Topics include: Predictive maintenance vs. reactive infrastructure management, Regional cooperation and shared technology services, Democratic accountability and citizen understanding of AI decisions, Digital divide in rural civic engagement, Cost-benefit analysis of municipal AI adoption. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. item.title: Cariboo Signals - Working Lands And Industry
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] A Ukrainian drone pilot found a hidden Russian arsenal, only to discover it was filled with horses and old cars and [GN AI ML Infrastructure] How clean energy infrastructure can catch up with the AI boom, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Whether increasing technology dependence in resource industries like ranching, forestry, and mining will ultimately benefit or harm rural working communities in the Cariboo region. Topics include: Battery reliability and maintenance infrastructure gaps, Competition between data centers and resource industries for electricity, Local technical capacity and repair capabilities, Economic diversification versus traditional industry stability, Technology as amplification versus replacement of human expertise. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. item.title: Cariboo Signals - Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.
NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] VIDEO: Vigil for Tumbler Ridge in Langley City and [The Guardian Global Development] ‘The goal has been to demystify’: how a colonial Nairobi library was restored and given back to the people, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive into arts, culture & digital storytelling, discussing how rural and remote communities can thoughtfully adopt and adapt emerging technologies.
Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
Sources discussed: 1. Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] VIDEO: Vigil for Tumbler Ridge in... 2. item.title: Cariboo Signals - Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.
NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceAlert] Black Hole May Be '100 Trillion Times' More Powerful Than The Death Star and [XDA Developers] LocalSend replaced AirDrop for me, and it works across every device I own, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive into resilient rural futures, discussing how rural and remote communities can thoughtfully adopt and adapt emerging technologies.
Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
